D. 95

sourceQuestion:
A positive integer m in base 10 when represented in base 2 has the representation p and in base 3 has the representation q. We get p - q = 990 where the subtraction is done in base 10. Which of the following is necessarily true?

A. 9 <= m <= 13
B. 9 <= m <= 13
C. 6 <= m <= 8
D. m <= 6

parsed.stem: A positive integer m in base 10 when represented in base 2 has the representation p and in base 3 has the representation q. We get p - q = 990 where the subtraction is done in base 10. Which of the following is necessarily true?

sourceQuestion:

A. 39
B. 57
C. 66
D. 93

parsed.stem: The sum of the digits of a two digit number is 12. If the new number formed by reversing the digits is greater than the original number by 54, find the original number.

sourceQuestion:
A 'frabjous' number is defined as a 3-digit number with all digits odd, and no two adjacent digits being the same. For example, 135 is a frabjous number, while 133 is not. How many such frabjous numbers exist?

A. 125
B. 729
C. 60
D. 80

parsed.stem: A 'frabjous' number is defined as a 3-digit number with all digits odd, and no two adjacent digits being the same. For example, 135 is a frabjous number, while 133 is not. How many such frabjous numbers exist?

sourceQuestion:
If a prime number on division by 4 gives a remainder of 1, then that number can be expressed as:

A. sum of squares of two natural numbers
B. sum of cubes of two natural numbers
C. sum of square roots of two natural numbers
D. sum of cube roots of two natural numbers
sourceAnswer: A. sum of squares of two natural numbers

parsed.stem: If a prime number on division by 4 gives a remainder of 1, then that number can be expressed as:

options.text: sum of squares of two natural numbers
sum of cubes of two natural numbers
sum of square roots of two natural numbers
sum of cube roots of two natural numbers

C. 2

A. a^2 - b
B. a^2 + b^2 + 1
C. a^2 + b + 1
D. ab - b

D. 19

sourceQuestion:
A house has a number which needs to be identified. The following three statements are given:
i.   If the house number is a multiple of 3, then it is a number from 50 to 59.
ii.  If the house number is NOT a multiple of 4, then it is a number from 60 to 69.
iii. If the house number is NOT a multiple of 6, then it is a number from 70 to 79.

What is the house number?

A. 54
B. 65
C. 66
D. 76

parsed.stem:
A house has a number which needs to be identified. The following three statements are given:
i.   If the house number is a multiple of 3, then it is a number from 50 to 59.
ii.  If the house number is NOT a multiple of 4, then it is a number from 60 to 69.
iii. If the house number is NOT a multiple of 6, then it is a number from 70 to 79.

What is the house number?

sourceQuestion:

parsed.stem: Given two sets X = {1, 2, 3} and Y = {2, 3, 4}, we construct a set Z of all possible fractions x/y where the numerators belong to set X and the denominators belong to set Y. The product of elements having minimum and maximum values in the set Z is _____.

sourceQuestion:
There are two candidates P and Q in an election. During the campaign, 80% of the voters promised to vote for P and the rest for Q. However, on the day of election, 10% of the voters went back on their promise to vote for P and instead voted for Q. Of the voters who had promised to vote for Q, 20% went back on their promise and instead voted for P. Suppose the total number of voters is 100, then what is the percentage of votes that P gets?

A. 100  B. 110  C. 90  D. 95

parsed.stem: There are two candidates P and Q in an election. During the campaign, 80% of the voters promised to vote for P and the rest for Q. However, on the day of election, 10% of the voters went back on their promise to vote for P and instead voted for Q. Of the voters who had promised to vote for Q, 20% went back on their promise and instead voted for P. Suppose the total number of voters is 100, then what is the percentage of votes that P gets?
